steps.title: Convert the percentage increase to a growth factor
steps.content: An increase of 4.5% per year means the value is multiplied by $(1 + 0.045) = 1.045$ each year. The house keeps 100% of its value and gains 4.5%, so the factor is 1.045, not 0.045 or 0.45.

steps.title: Write the exponential function
steps.content:
Starting value: \$181,000. After $x$ years of compounding at factor 1.045:
$$f(x) = 181{,}000(1.045)^x$$

steps.title: Match to the choices
steps.content:
• A: $f(x)=181,000(0.045)^x$ — base 0.045 < 1, so the value would collapse to nearly zero. Eliminated.
  • B: $f(x)=181,000(0.45)^x$ — base 0.45 models an annual loss of 55%. Eliminated.
  • C: $f(x)=181,000(1.045)^x$ — base 1.045 matches a 4.5% annual increase. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D: $f(x)=181,000(1.45)^x$ — base 1.45 would be a 45% annual increase, not 4.5%. Eliminated.
